cit.basis: Flat 35% corporate income tax (Income Tax Act, Cap. 123) at company level.

imputation_refund.note: Full-imputation system: shareholders may claim a refund of the company-level tax upon distribution, typically 6/7 of the tax on trading income, reducing the effective burden to ~5% for non-resident shareholders. The refund is shareholder-level; the company always pays the full 35%.

cit.note: Other refund fractions apply to specific income streams (e.g. 5/7 for passive interest/royalties, 2/3 where double-tax relief is claimed) — out of scope for this SME estimator.

vat.note: 18% standard VAT rate; reduced rates of 12%, 7% and 5% apply. EU One-Stop-Shop (OSS) available for cross-border B2C supplies.

pillar_two.note: Malta elected the 6-year deferral (Art. 50 EU Pillar Two Directive) and has not introduced an IIR/UTPR/QDMTT for the transitional period; in-scope groups (consolidated revenue >= EUR 750m) may instead be taxed under other jurisdictions' rules. Out of scope for this SME estimator.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Refund fractions vary by income stream (6/7, 5/7, 2/3, 100% participation exemption); shareholder residence, anti-abuse and treaty rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official CFR guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
